name: Esparragosa de Lares from Puebla de Alcocer – circular by the Sierra del Castillo
name_original: Esparragosa de Lares desde Puebla de Alcocer – circular por la Sierra del Castillo
description: Hike a moderate 7.4-mile circular route from Esparragosa de Lares to Puebla de Alcocer, featuring castle views and the La Serena reservoir.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Puebla De Alcocer traverse a diverse and rugged landscape in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura, Spain. The region is characterized by extensive untouched land, slate soils, and the prominent La Serena and Zújar Reservoirs. Hikers can explore hills, sierras like Sierra del Castillo and Sierra de los Lares, and the unique conical Cerro Masatrigo. These features provide varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Puebla De Alcocer The most popular hiking route is Cerro Masatrigo - circular via the botanical trail (SL-BA 198), a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends the striking conical mountain, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador Celeste de Galizuela from Puebla de Alcocer – round trip via the Castillo and the Embalse de la Serena, a moderate 8.7 miles (14.0 km) path. This route provides vistas over the vast La Serena Reservoir and passes by the historic castle. Local hikers also love the Esparragosa de Lares from Puebla de Alcocer – circular by the Sierra del Castillo, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra del Castillo, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Puebla De Alcocer is defined by large reservoirs, historic castles perched on hills, and rugged sierras. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents. The routes in Puebla De Alcocer are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Puebla De Alcocer's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Puebla de Alcocer?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails around Puebla de Alcocer, offering a variety of experiences. These include 2 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 4 more challenging paths, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991604893/cerro-masatrigo-circular-via-the-botanical-trail-sl-ba-198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo - circular via the botanical trail (SL-BA 198)&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging ascent with panoramic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102754200/esparragosa-de-lares-from-puebla-de-alcocer-circular-by-the-sierra-del-castillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esparragosa de Lares from Puebla de Alcocer – circular by the Sierra del Castillo&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the scenic Sierra del Castillo.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking?
A: The region boasts diverse and rugged natural landscapes. You'll encounter extensive untouched land, slate soils, and prominent features like the vast La Serena and Zújar Reservoirs. Hikers can explore hills, sierras such as Sierra del Castillo and Sierra de los Lares, and the unique conical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails that pass through Puebla de Alcocer?
A: Yes, portions of the Camino Natural del Guadiana (GR-114), a long-distance trail, traverse this region. These sections offer routes along the Orellana reservoir, winding through oak groves and traditional dehesas.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I visit while hiking?
A: Many trails offer access to historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5460742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt;, perched atop a hill, provides captivating panoramic views. You might also encounter the Ermita de la Cueva near the castle, adding to the historical appeal. The town itself features traditional architecture and historic religious buildings like the Convent of San Francisco and the Church of Santiago.

Q: Are there any routes with impressive viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101692844/mirador-celeste-de-galizuela-from-puebla-de-alcocer-round-trip-via-the-castillo-and-the-embalse-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Celeste de Galizuela from Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; route is specifically known for its picturesque vistas over the vast La Serena Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt; also offers rewarding panoramic views from its peak.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Puebla de Alcocer?
A: The routes in Puebla de Alcocer are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 8 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning reservoir views, and the opportunity to explore historic sites.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are 2 easy routes available. These are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them suitable for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed walk. We recommend checking individual route details for specific suitability.

Q: Can I combine hiking with water activities in the area?
A: Yes, the proximity of the La Serena and Orellana reservoirs provides ample opportunities for various water-based leisure activities, especially during the warmer months. Many hiking trails offer views of these large bodies of water.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Puebla de Alcocer?
A: The region's diverse landscapes can be enjoyed throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures, ideal for longer hikes. During warmer months, the reservoirs provide opportunities for water activities to complement your hiking experience.

Q: Are there any specific natural features to look out for?
A: Keep an eye out for the striking, almost perfectly conical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt;, encircled by the La Serena Reservoir. The extensive slate soils and the vastness of the La Serena and Zújar Reservoirs are also defining natural characteristics of the area.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching while hiking?
A: Yes, the area promotes birding, with events like 'Birding Puebla' catering to ornithology enthusiasts. The natural habitats around the reservoirs and dehesas make it a good spot for observing various bird species.
tips: Esparragosa de Lares sits on the western edge of the area known as La Siberia, a name that refers not to the harshness of the climate, but to the distance from the capital of the province. In front of the town the immense plains of the Zújar valley open up, being a landscape of great natural beauty due to its grandeur. Part of these spaces; Before meadows of holm oaks and cork oaks, pastures and barren areas, they are covered by the waters of the Zújar dam and the gigantic La Serena dam.

The castle of Puebla de Alcocer is an imposing castle built during the 12th and 13th centuries, remodeled in the 15th century by Don Gutierre de Sotomayor, Master of the Order of Alcántara, thanks to the granting of a license from the King of Castile, Juan II.

It is located on an elongated rocky mass, at the highest point of Puebla de Alcocer, from where the movements of the adversaries were monitored for kilometers around. Today the regions of La Serena and La Siberia are contemplated and the fortification can be seen from several points.

Puebla de Alcocer is a small town in the province of Badajoz that belongs to the Comarca de la Siberia Extremadura.

The population center is located in the mountains, crowned by a medieval castle and surrounded by two of the largest reservoirs in Spain: La Serena and Orellana, being one of the most unique and beautiful enclaves in the entire province.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll start on wide rural paths, moving into landscapes of dehesa and low scrub. As you approach Puebla de Alcocer, expect olive groves and gentle slopes. The ascent to the Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer involves a more demanding, somewhat steep, and often cobbled path. The return route typically features smooth descents through jara, holm oaks, and rocky areas.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail offers a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. Key highlights include the impressive Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer, which provides spectacular panoramic views, especially of the vast La Serena reservoir. You'll also pass the Parish Church of Esparragosa de Lares and the historic Ermita de la Virgen de la Cueva, nestled among holm oaks and olive groves.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is generally considered to have a moderate difficulty, with some sections being more demanding. While initial parts have gentle slopes, the final climb to the Puebla de Alcocer Castle is described as 'exacting.' It's best suited for hikers seeking a varied and somewhat challenging walk, rather than absolute beginners or very young children, unless they are experienced hikers.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, the trail typically starts from Esparragosa de Lares or Puebla de Alcocer. Look for designated parking areas near the town centers or the beginning of rural paths leading out of these villages. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beautiful scenery year-round. However, for the most comfortable experience, consider hiking in spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers in Extremadura can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable if visiting during that season. Winter can also be pleasant, but be prepared for cooler temperatures.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Esparragosa de Lares from Puebla de Alcocer circular trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on this trail. However, given the varied terrain, including sections through dehesa and scrubland, and the presence of livestock in rural areas, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect local wildlife and property.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this circular trail. It traverses public paths and natural areas, making it freely accessible to all hikers.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. Notably, it follows parts of the historic Sendero del Santero, which makes up about 20% of the route. You'll also find it intersects with smaller portions of the Camino Natural del Guadiana and various local paths like Camino de la Ermita and Paseo de Los Pilaritos.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: Hikers can expect spectacular panoramic views throughout the route. As you ascend towards the Puebla de Alcocer Castle, you'll be treated to grand vistas of the vast La Serena reservoir. The trail also offers beautiful panoramas of the surrounding valleys and the natural beauty of the 'Siberia Extremeña' region, especially from the Ermita de la Virgen de la Cueva.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Both Esparragosa de Lares and Puebla de Alcocer are small towns that offer basic amenities. You should be able to find cafes, bars, and restaurants in either village to refuel before or after your hike. It's a good idea to check opening hours, especially if hiking during off-peak seasons.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate to demanding nature of the trail and varied terrain, we recommend sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water (especially in warmer months as water sources on the trail are limited), sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks. A map or GPS device is also advisable, though the route is generally well-marked.

waypoints.name: Parish Church of Esparragosa de Lares
